findings.title: CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 (non-commercial) philos derivatives are committed to git and actively used by the test suite
findings.description: The R/V Philos dataset is recorded in data/README.md (line 18, 347, 380) as CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 — NonCommercial + ShareAlike, exactly the license class the project deliberately rejected for MOANA and Global Fishing Watch. Despite this, a substantial body of philos-DERIVED artifacts is tracked in git and consumed by tracked tests: the three video-derived existence-label fixtures (tests/fixtures/philos/labels/{car_carrier_near,close_approach,sunset_cruise}_labels.csv, each header states 'Video-derived ... labels for the philos <clip>'), tests/fixtures/philos/charts/radar_structure_points.geojson (Boston radar-clutter points extracted from philos radar), charts/philos_cluster_classification.csv/.png and charts/philos_chart_coverage.png, plus ~10 docs/baselines/*philos*.csv metric dumps. tests/replay/test_philos_*_labels.cpp and PhilosLabelReplay.hpp validate the tracker against these NC-SA-derived labels. Adaptations of CC-BY-NC-SA material inherit NonCommercial + ShareAlike, so shipping them in a repo whose stated goal is commercial usability is a licensing conflict, and the ShareAlike term would virally attach to anything built from them. This also flatly contradicts tests/fixtures/philos/README.md's own invariant ('Everything under tests/fixtures/ is gitignored ... never committed'), which is the mechanism the team relies on to keep NC data out of git.
findings.evidence: `git ls-files | grep philos` returns tests/fixtures/philos/labels/*.csv (3), tests/fixtures/philos/charts/radar_structure_points.geojson, charts/philos_cluster_classification.csv/.png, charts/philos_chart_coverage.png, docs/baselines/*philos*.csv (~10), and tests/replay/test_philos_*_labels.cpp. data/README.md:18 '| philos/ | ... | CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 | ... |'. Label CSV headers begin '# Video-derived existence/region labels for the philos ... clip'. tests/fixtures/philos/README.md:176 asserts fixtures are 'never committed' — contradicted by the above.
findings.suggested_action: Treat philos exactly like the rejected MOANA/GFW datasets: remove all philos-derived committed artifacts (labels, charts geojson, charts/philos_* csv/png, docs/baselines philos CSVs) and the tests that depend on them from git history for the commercial release, OR obtain a commercial-use license from MIT Sea Grant. At minimum, do not ship NC-SA derivatives in a commercially-licensed library.

verdict.reasoning: Every factual element of the claim reproduced independently. (1) LICENSE: data/README.md:18 records philos/ as CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 (NonCommercial+ShareAlike), the same class the project rejected for MOANA/GFW. (2) COMMITTED DERIVATIVES: git ls-files --error-unmatch confirms tests/fixtures/philos/labels/{car_carrier_near,close_approach,sunset_cruise}_labels.csv and tests/fixtures/philos/charts/radar_structure_points.geojson are genuinely tracked; their headers self-identify as philos-derived ('# Video-derived existence/region labels for the philos <clip> clip ... operator video + frame/radar cross-reference pass'). Also tracked: charts/philos_cluster_classification.csv/.png, charts/philos_chart_coverage.png, ~10 docs/baselines/*philos*.csv. (3) FORCE-ADDED: .gitignore:29 '/tests/fixtures/*' ignores everything with explicit ! exceptions ONLY for sim_multisensor and rbad generators — no philos exception, so these were git add -f'd past the ignore rule (commit 6151f50 for car_carrier_near). (4) SELF-CONTRADICTION: tests/fixtures/philos/README.md:175 states 'Everything under tests/fixtures/ is gitignored (local, never committed)' — directly contradicted. (5) TEST CONSUMPTION: PhilosLabelReplay.hpp:368 loads the geojson; test_philos_sunset_labels.cpp:46 and test_philos_close_approach_labels.cpp:40 load the CSVs via loadLabels(). The only nuance is whether bare existence/region labels and extracted coordinates are legally copyrightable adaptations vs. uncopyrightable facts — a genuine edge case — but the project's own stated commercial-usability goal, its explicit prior rejection of NC-SA datasets, and its own README invariant make committing philos-derived artifacts a real license/policy conflict regardless. Matches the task quality bar (license terms incompatible with commercial use, with committed provenance-carrying derivatives).

findings.title: 683 MB of CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial) Pohang data present on disk — same license class the project rejected
findings.description: data/pohang/ holds 683 MB of the Pohang Canal Dataset (pohang00 + pohang02, each radar.zip/navigation.zip/calibration.zip). data/README.md:195 states the license is 'CC-BY-NC-4.0. Non-commercial only.' This is the identical non-commercial constraint that caused MOANA and GFW to be rejected as commercially-incompatible. The raw zips are not git-tracked (data/ is gitignored, confirmed by `git ls-files | grep pohang` = empty), so this is not a git-history blocker, but keeping non-commercial data staged on disk in a commercially-intended project is a consistency/cleanup risk (it can be inadvertently committed or leaked, and the pohang_radar/pohang_lidar fixture READMEs do not restate the NC restriction).
findings.evidence: `du -sh data/pohang` => 683M; data/pohang/pohang00 and pohang02 each contain radar.zip/navigation.zip/calibration.zip. data/README.md:195 'License: CC-BY-NC-4.0. Non-commercial only.' `git ls-files | grep pohang` returns nothing (untracked but present).
findings.suggested_action: Decide explicitly whether Pohang is allowed at all given the commercial constraint; if kept for internal research only, isolate it out of the project tree and note the NC restriction in the pohang fixture READMEs. If not, delete data/pohang to match the MOANA/GFW precedent.

findings.title: Singapore Maritime Dataset has NO recorded license or provenance and is absent from the data manifest
findings.description: The SMD-based fixture (tests/fixtures/smd_eo/) emits 36 bearing/ObjectGT CSVs (on disk under out/) derived from Singapore Maritime Dataset on-shore annotations, fetched via a Google Drive gdown ID and a rarlab unrar binary. Neither the fixture README nor data/README.md records any license for SMD — `grep -niE 'smd|singapore' data/README.md` returns nothing, so SMD is entirely missing from the source-of-truth provenance manifest that claims to cover every source. SMD (Prasad et al., NUS) is a research dataset whose distribution terms are non-commercial/research-oriented and are neither cited nor verified here. Using its derived annotations in the evaluation bundle with zero license provenance is a legal-risk and provenance gap.
findings.evidence: `grep -niE 'smd|singapore' data/README.md` => no matches (manifest claims to cover all sources but omits SMD). tests/fixtures/smd_eo/README.md has no license section; fetch is `gdown 1HnHyQzhzzDlYh15y9_K1mNZX3grlSDMM` + rarlab unrar. tests/fixtures/smd_eo/out/ contains ~36 *_ObjectGT.csv derived files on disk.
findings.suggested_action: Add SMD to data/README.md with its actual license and citation, and confirm those terms permit commercial use before relying on SMD-derived fixtures; if the terms are non-commercial, treat it like pohang/philos.

findings.title: trondheim_harbor.geojson is OSM/ODbL (share-alike database license) committed to git
findings.description: tests/fixtures/autoferry/trondheim_harbor.geojson is tracked in git and its own properties record source 'OpenStreetMap via Overpass API, (c) OpenStreetMap contributors, ODbL'. ODbL is commercial-use-friendly but carries an attribution requirement (satisfied here, in-file) and a share-alike obligation on derivative databases. Bundling an ODbL-derived land polygon into the product is acceptable, but the share-alike term is worth flagging so an integrator does not unknowingly incur database-license obligations. Attribution is present, so this is hygiene, not a blocker.
findings.evidence: tests/fixtures/autoferry/trondheim_harbor.geojson properties.source = 'OpenStreetMap via Overpass API, (c) OpenStreetMap contributors, ODbL'; file is in `git ls-files tests/fixtures`.
findings.suggested_action: Note the ODbL share-alike/attribution obligation in the integration/licensing docs so consumers who redistribute the derived database comply; no code change needed.

units.coverage_notes:
Cross-checked every dataset root against data/README.md (the stated provenance manifest) and against `git ls-files tests/fixtures data charts`.

CLEAN / commercial-OK (verified):
- charts/ NOAA ENC: US5BOSCC.zip/US5BOSCD.zip/US5BOSDC.zip and ENC_ROOT/* are tracked; charts/ENC_ROOT/USERAGREEMENT.TXT confirms NOAA ENCs 'may be downloaded, used, and redistributed without restriction' (US Government product). Redistribution guideline (display agreement + reference) is met by the included USERAGREEMENT.TXT. charts/ANALYSIS.md documents S-57 GDAL parsing provenance. charts/geojson/* are derived from these public-domain ENCs. Clean.
- tests/fixtures/static/harbor_obstacles.geojson: tiny hand-authored synthetic ENC-style obstacles (source_id 'ENC:wreck-1'), no external data. Clean.
- tests/fixtures/rbad/: only generator/*.py + README + requirements tracked (data local-only); README records Zenodo doi:10.5281/zenodo.16936465, CC-BY-4.0 (commercial-OK). Clean.
- tests/fixtures/sim_multisensor/: fully synthetic generator (generator/*.py + README + lockfile tracked); self-produced, no license concern. Clean.
- data/dlr (HAXR): 1.4 GB on disk, not tracked; data/README records DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19824555, CC-BY-4.0 (commercial-OK). AIS is anonymised/derived. Note: the tests/fixtures/haxr_cfar/README.md itself carries no license line (provenance lives only in data/README) — minor, not raised as a finding.
- data/marinecadastre (US public domain), data/dma (DMA open data / figshare mirror doi:10.6084/m9.figshare.11577543), data/kystverket (NLOD), data/stonesoup (Stone-Soup MIT, LICENSE present; Solent AIS demo) — all commercial-compatible per data/README and confirmed not to be NC. None tracked in git (data/ gitignored).
- data/autoferry: CC-BY-4.0 per README; not tracked.

VERIFIED NOT git-tracked (so no git-history licensing blocker): pohang, smd, dlr, dma, kystverket, marinecadastre, stonesoup, autoferry raw data, and the smd_eo/pohang_*/haxr_cfar fixture out/ CSVs — data/ and fixture out/ are gitignored.

Confirmed on-disk sizes: data/pohang 683M (NC), data/dlr 1.4G, data/dma 266M, data/marinecadastre 277M, data/stonesoup 59M, data/kystverket 15M, data/autoferry 7.1M; data/philos and data/smd are empty (cleaned up after extraction) — but philos-DERIVED products are committed (see high finding) and smd-derived CSVs sit in tests/fixtures/smd_eo/out/.

Not inspected in depth: raw HDF5/CSV column-level stats of dlr/dma/marinecadastre (out of the licensing-provenance unit's scope — belongs to the loader/data-quality auditors). .venv/ and site-packages/ under tests/fixtures/philos ignored per instructions.

findings.title: philos fixtures are CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 (non-commercial) derivatives, used to validate a commercial product
findings.description: data/README.md and tests/fixtures/philos/README.md both state the MIT Sea Grant R/V Philos dataset is licensed CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 (NonCommercial + ShareAlike), form-gated with an 'intended use' question. Every philos fixture the test suite depends on is a derivative of that recording: ownship.csv, ais.csv, radar_plots.csv, camera_bearings.csv (from the bag video+GPS+IMU+AIS+radar) and the labels/*.csv (README: 'Video-derived ... from operator video'). All seven test_philos_* tests validate navtracker against these derivatives. NonCommercial forbids use in support of a commercial product regardless of git redistribution; this exactly matches the project's own established NO-GO precedent on NC data (memory: GFW CC-BY-NC 4.0 CLOSED NO-GO, MOANA rejected; Pohang CC-BY-NC-4.0 'Non-commercial only'). The user org (tkmsgroup.com / ThyssenKrupp Marine Systems) is commercial, making the entire philos validation arm a licensing blocker for a pre-release.
findings.evidence: data/README.md provenance table row 'philos/ ... CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 Form-gated' and section 'MIT Sea Grant R/V Philos (philos/) — NOT FETCHED ... CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0, distributed only through a download form'; tests/fixtures/philos/README.md 'R/V Philos ... MIT Sea Grant'; labels/*.csv headers 'Video-derived existence/region labels ... operator video'.
findings.suggested_action: Treat the philos validation arm as non-commercial-only: gate/exclude test_philos_* from any commercial-release evaluation, or obtain a commercial-use grant from MIT Sea Grant. Apply the same NO-GO rule already used for GFW/MOANA/Pohang.

verdict.reasoning:
I independently reproduced every load-bearing observation in the claim.

LICENSE (the core finding): data/README.md line 18 provenance table lists `philos/ ... CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 ... Form-gated`. The dedicated section (lines 345-368) states verbatim: "CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0, distributed only through a download form ... The form asks for name, email, organisation, and intended use". CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 carries the NonCommercial + ShareAlike restriction. Confirmed.

DERIVATIVES: tests/fixtures/philos/README.md documents that the extractors turn the R/V Philos `section.bag`/`radar_pcd` (MIT Sea Grant, form-gated) into ais.csv, ownship.csv, radar_plots.csv, camera_bearings.csv. I confirmed the extracted CSVs exist under tests/fixtures/philos/out/ais_ferry_near/ (ais.csv, ownship.csv, radar_plots.csv, camera_bearings.csv, meta.txt, radar_truth.csv). The labels/*.csv headers confirm the "Video-derived ... operator video" provenance: e.g. car_carrier_near_labels.csv reads "# Video-derived existence/region labels ... Produced by the ... operator video + frame/radar cross-reference pass". These are all derivatives of the NC recording.

TEST DEPENDENCE: I found 9 philos test files (tests/replay/test_philos_los_shadow_probe, _occupancy_coverage_6c, _close_approach_labels, _farcross, _sunset_labels, _close_approach_cpa, _ospa, plus tests/benchmark/test_philos_cluttermap_ab, and the PhilosLabelReplay.hpp harness). The claim's "seven test_philos_* tests" undercounts, but that strengthens rather than weakens the finding.

COMMERCIAL CONTEXT: user email is andreas.klots@tkmsgroup.com (ThyssenKrupp Marine Systems, a commercial shipbuilder); this is a pre-release review of the product.

PRECEDENT: the project's own established stance treats NC data as a blocker — data/README.md itself flags Pohang as "CC-BY-NC-4.0. Non-commercial only", and project memory records GFW CC-BY-NC 4.0 CLOSED NO-GO and MOANA rejected on the same grounds. NonCommercial restricts USE (not merely git redistribution), so gitignoring the fixtures does not cure it — the test suite actively consumes the derivatives, and the fixtures are present and live in this main tree.

Adversarial checks for refutation: (1) Is it out of scope? No — "license terms incompatible with commercial use" is explicitly in the auditor's quality bar. (2) Is there a mitigating license grant? None found; the README even instructs requesting the data under an "academic research" pretext, which conflicts with commercial product validation. (3) Count discrepancy (7 vs 9 tests) is immaterial. No basis to refute; the licensing incompatibility is real and well-supported.

findings.title: OSPA truth is the full AIS report set, but 22 of 23 targets lie far beyond radar coverage
findings.description: test_philos_ospa.cpp scores OSPA with truth_as_measurement = the entire loadAisCsv() output (runConfig(radar, ais, ...) and runConfig(both, ais, ...)), applying a 100 m cutoff over 1 s windows with no range/coverage filter. In ais_ferry_near only ONE of 23 AIS truth MMSIs (367074170, ~84-92 m) is within the radar's reach; the other 22 sit 1.3-15.8 km away and radar_plots.csv max range is ~976 m, so radar-only config A can never detect them. Every OSPA window therefore accrues ~22 unavoidable misses pinned at the 100 m cap, so the printed 'mean OSPA' is a structural coverage artifact, not a tracking-accuracy figure, and the small real signal (fusion on the one near target) is swamped. The test does not gate on OSPA (only EXPECT_GT unique_ids/n_windows > 0) and comments that it is 'detector- and lifecycle-dominated', but the number is emitted as 'the deliverable' and can mislead anyone comparing it to true-GT datasets.
findings.evidence: radar_truth.csv per-uid range: ais_ferry_near = 22/23 targets 1440-15804 m OUT of ~976 m radar coverage, only 367074170 (84-92 m) IN; test_philos_ospa.cpp evalWindow builds truth_now from all truth_as_measurement within +/-0.5 s with ospaGreedy(...,100.0), and runConfig is called with the unfiltered `ais` vector.
findings.suggested_action: Restrict the OSPA truth to targets inside radar coverage (or annotate the printed number as coverage-limited), so the philos OSPA figure is not read as an accuracy comparison against true-GT benchmarks.

findings.title: Validated camera-bearing fixtures are never exercised by any scenario test
findings.description: The camera-bearing pipeline has no scenario-level regression coverage. Every philos runClip() call passes load_camera=false (test_philos_occupancy_coverage_6c.cpp lines 178-579, test_philos_los_shadow_probe.cpp lines 246-250), so PhilosLabelReplay's camera path (buildCameraFrames / loadCameraBearingsCsv) is dead in the label-scored tests. sunset_cruise/camera_bearings.csv (4605 rows, README-marked 'center only (if sanity passes)') is consumed by nothing. Only ais_ferry_near/camera_bearings.csv is touched, and solely by the loader smoke test (test_camera_bearing_loader.cpp), which asserts mechanics only (no accuracy, by the stated circularity rule). Separately, car_carrier_near carries _camera_detections.csv but no camera_bearings.csv, so any future load_camera=true on that clip silently no-ops (runClip guards on fileExists). Not a data defect, but the shipped/validated camera bearings have no end-to-end guard.
findings.evidence: grep 'runClip(' in test_philos_*.cpp shows no load_camera=true; ls out/sunset_cruise has camera_bearings.csv (unused); ls out/car_carrier_near has _camera_detections.csv but no camera_bearings.csv; PhilosLabelReplay.hpp runClipInputs only loads camera when load_camera && fileExists(camera_csv).
findings.suggested_action: Add at least one label-scored run with load_camera=true on a camera-bearing clip (sunset_cruise or ais_ferry_near), or document that camera coverage is intentionally loader-only.

units.coverage_notes: Cross-checked all four loaders against the data. CLEAN items: (1) Timestamps — every file in every clip is monotonic (loaders also sort by time) and shares one common unix epoch/clock within a clip; ownship/radar/camera/ais start within ~0.3 s and end within ~0.13 s of each other in ais_ferry_near (694.00-713.96 vs 693.94-713.83 vs 694.06-713.92 vs 694.36-713.81), so no silent inter-sensor clock offset. Duplicate timestamps in radar_plots/camera are multiple returns per scan (expected); the loaders handle them. (2) Radar azimuth convention — radar_plots.csv `azimuth_deg` is BODY-relative marine (N=0 CW), which is what loadPlotCsvBodyFrame and loadRadarTruthCsv assume (heading+az). Verified numerically against AIS: target 316028326 heading 239.777 + az 237.44 wraps to 117.2 deg true (computed true bearing 117.4 deg), range 7833 m vs computed 7830 m; near ferry 367074170 heading+6.3=246.1 vs computed 245.9. loadPlotCsv (station frame) would misread these as absolute, but NO philos test uses it. (3) Camera bearing convention — CameraBearingCsvReader reads `bearing_rel_deg` as relative-to-heading, deg->rad, value=atan2(dN,dE); fixtures are consistent (right cam +23..+72, center ~0, left negative); test HeadingCompositionAndValueConvention asserts exactly this math. README documents ais_ferry_near center as AIS-RANSAC validated (residual median 0.45 deg / p90 1.32 deg). (4) close_approach correctly has NO camera_bearings.csv (README: intrinsics REFUSED for the prodromos vessel); no test loads camera from it. (5) Value plausibility — ownship lat/lon in Boston harbor (~42.37,-71.05), heading 0-360; radar range 54-976 m, sig<=0 rows fall back to loader defaults (25 m / 1 deg); AIS sog 0.05-12.6 m/s, cog 0-360, lat/lon in-basin; no NaN/blank in critical columns (finite-value counts equal row counts). (6) radar_truth uid == ais mmsi (derived reprojection). (7) Zero-AIS clips (sunset/close_approach/car_carrier/almost_cross/sailboats) correctly have header-only ais.csv, matching test expectations (farcross asserts almost_cross has no radar_truth). Minor unreported nit: the first radar plot in every clip precedes the first ownship pose by 60-260 ms and is dropped by poseAtOrBefore (negligible, first partial scan). NOT verified: could not open the source .bag/.pcd (data/philos is empty, form-gated) so I could not confirm the CSVs reproduce from the raw recording; extractor scripts (extract_section.py/extract_radar.py/extract_camera_bearings.py) were not line-audited.

findings.dataset: haxr (DLR HAXR / Hamburg port radar, data/dlr + tests/fixtures/haxr_cfar)
findings.title: No license / provenance / attribution recorded for the DLR HAXR dataset that all HAXR tests validate against
findings.description: The HAXR radar+AIS dataset is the primary real-radar validation source for the whole haxr unit (kattwyk/parkhafen/seemannshoeft HDF5+AIS+stations), consumed by test_haxr_ospa, test_los_guard_haxr_ab, and test_veto_isolation_haxr_ab. Its manifest records ONLY per-file SHA256 hashes — no source URL, no license, no citation, no commercial-use terms. There is no LICENSE file anywhere under data/dlr, and no docs/ page documents the dataset's origin or terms. navtracker is a commercial product (tkmsgroup) and the team has explicitly rejected other datasets for non-commercial licensing (memory: D7 MOANA, D10 GFW = CC BY-NC). For the flagship real-radar dataset the commercial-use terms are simply unknown, which is a release-gating question until resolved. (Mitigation: data/dlr and the generated out/ are gitignored, so the data is not distributed with the repo — this is a use/verification blocker, not proven redistribution of restricted data.)
findings.evidence: data/dlr/manifest.json contains only {station, split_hour_utc, radar.sha256, ais.sha256} entries and a stations ref — no license/source field (tail + grep -niE 'licen|copyright|cc-by|doi|source' returns nothing). `ls data/dlr/*LICENSE*` = none. grep of docs/ for HAXR provenance/license terms returns nothing.
findings.suggested_action: Record the DLR HAXR dataset's source, retrieval date, and license/usage terms (manifest field + a docs/datasets provenance entry) and confirm commercial-use permission before relying on these numbers for a release baseline.

verdict.reasoning:
REFUTED. The claim's core assertions — no license, no source URL, no citation, "commercial-use terms are simply unknown," and "no docs/ page documents the dataset's origin or terms" — are all false. The auditor scoped the license search too narrowly (only data/dlr/manifest.json, `ls data/dlr/*LICENSE*`, and grep of docs/) and missed data/README.md, which sits one directory up and is explicitly the provenance record.

Confirmed sub-observations (auditor is right on these narrow points): data/dlr/manifest.json contains ONLY {station, split_hour_utc, radar.sha256, ais.sha256} entries plus a stations ref — `grep -niE 'licen|copyright|cc-by|doi|source|url|attribut|citation' data/dlr/manifest.json` returns nothing; and there is no data/dlr/*LICENSE* file. But the manifest.json is a hash-integrity file by design, not the provenance record.

The provenance IS recorded, in data/README.md (present in this main tree), which states in its header: "Files are not committed (see .gitignore); this README is the source-of-truth manifest." That README documents HAXR fully:
- License table row (line 12): `dlr/` | X-band radar (polar HDF5) + AIS (CSV) | **CC-BY-4.0** | 1.4 GB | Fetched.
- Dedicated section (lines 94-135): "## DLR HAXR — Hamburg port X-band radar + AIS (`dlr/`)  DOI: `10.5281/zenodo.19824555`. Published Feb 2026 by DLR Institute of Communications and Navigation (Meinert, Moreira)." with fetch URLs (https://zenodo.org/api/records/19824555/...), sensor-stack/column descriptions, and lineage to the Fowdur/Baum/Heymann 2021 dataset.

Crucially the recorded license is CC-BY-4.0 (attribution-only, commercial-use permitted), NOT a non-commercial license. This is the same README that records the datasets the team rejected for NC terms (pohang CC-BY-NC-4.0, philos CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0), so the distinction is deliberately captured. The claim's premise that HAXR's commercial-use terms are "unknown" and a "release-gating question" is contradicted by the explicit CC-BY-4.0 record.

Note (does not rescue the claim): data/README.md is not git-tracked (`git ls-files --error-unmatch data/README.md` fails; the whole data/ tree is gitignored, though data/.gitignore whitelists README.md via `!README.md`). So provenance is not *distributed with the repo* — but the auditor's own claim already concedes the data is gitignored and frames this as a use/verification blocker, not redistribution. For use/verification, the terms are plainly recorded in the tree alongside the data (CC-BY-4.0, DOI, authors, source URL). The factual claim "no license/provenance/attribution recorded" is wrong.

findings.title: Flagship real-OSPA-on-real-radar test uses CWD-relative fixture paths and silently GTEST_SKIPs under ctest
findings.description: test_haxr_ospa (the only test that streams HAXR plots through the tracker and reports OSPA vs AIS truth) resolves its fixtures via bare relative paths and, unlike the two AB tests, does NOT anchor them with NAVTRACKER_SOURCE_DIR. ctest / gtest_discover_tests run from build/, where those relative paths do not resolve, so fileExists() fails and the test hits GTEST_SKIP. The fixtures are present in the source tree, but the test only actually runs when invoked from the repo root. Result: the primary HAXR accuracy test is effectively never exercised in normal CI, while its companions (test_los_guard_haxr_ab, test_veto_isolation_haxr_ab) DO run because they use srcAbs(NAVTRACKER_SOURCE_DIR). (This overlaps a T3 test-sufficiency finding but is squarely in the haxr scope.)
findings.evidence: test_haxr_ospa.cpp:38-41 kDefaultPlotsCsv="tests/fixtures/haxr_cfar/out/kattwyk_08_t40.csv", kAisCsv="data/dlr/kattwyk_08-UTC.csv" (relative); grep -c NAVTRACKER_SOURCE_DIR test_haxr_ospa.cpp = 0. Contrast test_los_guard_haxr_ab.cpp:44 srcAbs()/NAVTRACKER_SOURCE_DIR.
findings.suggested_action: Anchor the HAXR OSPA fixture paths with NAVTRACKER_SOURCE_DIR (as the AB tests do) so the test runs under ctest instead of silently skipping.

findings.title: Sparse AIS truth (10-20 s cadence) scored against a 1 Hz eval clock inflates OSPA to the cutoff on most windows
findings.description: test_haxr_ospa builds one OSPA snapshot per 1 s tick, taking as truth only AIS samples within +/-0.5 s of the tick. But AIS reports arrive every ~10-20 s per vessel, so the large majority of 1 Hz ticks have zero in-window truth. ospaGreedy with an empty truth set and N existing tracks returns the cutoff (n=max(|X|,|Y|), each surplus track costs cutoff^2), i.e. ~200 m for every such window. The reported 'mean OSPA' is therefore dominated by truth-empty windows and measures 'tracks exist when AIS was silent', not tracking accuracy; the EXPECT_LT(mean_ospa,200) guard is nearly vacuous. The truth cadence is native AIS and is fine — the defect is scoring it against a denser clock without hold/resample.
findings.evidence: test_haxr_ospa.cpp:106,113-119 (1 s ticks, +/-0.5 s truth gate). AIS per-vessel deltas measured on data/dlr/kattwyk_08-UTC.csv (uid 96443): 10 10 10 20 10 10 10 10 20 10 10 s. Ospa.cpp:13-14 returns max-cardinality cutoff penalty when one set is empty. README.md:61 states AIS cadence 8-12 s.
findings.suggested_action: Evaluate OSPA at AIS-report instants (or hold/resample truth to the eval clock, as HaxrScenarioRun's philos sibling does via resampleTruthToClock) so windows are scored against real in-window truth; report OSPA next to P_D per the README caveat.

findings.title: AIS-derived truth velocity hardcoded to zero for all vessels, including moving ones
findings.description: loadHaxrTruth sets every TruthSample.velocity to Vector2d::Zero() regardless of vessel motion. The AIS truth is range/azimuth position only, so velocity is simply not carried. This is inert for the current position-only OSPA/GOSPA scoring, but it is a latent misalignment: any velocity-aware truth consumer (velocity-OSPA, motion-model NEES vs truth, or a future SOG/COG accuracy metric) would score moving vessels against a false zero-velocity truth. Worth an explicit comment or a derived velocity so it is not silently mistaken for real ground-truth kinematics.
findings.suggested_action: Document that HAXR truth is position-only (velocity is a placeholder), or derive per-vessel velocity by finite-differencing consecutive AIS fixes so velocity-aware metrics are not misled.

units.coverage_notes:
Scope covered: loaders adapters/replay/HaxrTruthLoader.{hpp,cpp} and PlotCsvReplayAdapter.{hpp,cpp}; scenario wiring adapters/benchmark/ReplayScenarioRun.cpp (HaxrScenarioRun); tests tests/replay/test_haxr_ospa.cpp, tests/benchmark/test_los_guard_haxr_ab.cpp, test_veto_isolation_haxr_ab.cpp, test_occupancy_ab.cpp; data tests/fixtures/haxr_cfar/{README.md,.gitignore} and data/dlr/{stations.csv,manifest.json,*-UTC.csv}.

Verified CLEAN:
- Loader/data column+unit contract matches. Plot CSVs have the 8-column header tod,range_m,azimuth_deg,sigma_r_m,sigma_az_deg,n_cells,amp_max,station exactly as PlotCsvReplayAdapter reads (fields[0,1,2,3,4,7]); AIS CSVs have tod,uid,range(m),azimuth(deg) matching HaxrTruthLoader (fields[0..3]). tod = seconds-of-day UTC (kattwyk_08 AIS starts 28800=08:00:00), confirmed by README.
- Frame alignment is CORRECT: HaxrTruthLoader offsets truth by station_xy + range*[sin(az),cos(az)] with marine az (N=0, CW); PlotCsvReplayAdapter projects plots via projectRangeBearingToEnu which (Projection.hpp:15) offsets by (range*sinθ, range*cosθ) from the same station position. Truth and plots share one ENU frame per station, so no truth/sensor spatial misalignment.
- CIRCULARITY RULE HOLDS. Default HAXR path feeds radar plots only and scores vs AIS-derived truth (no AIS as sensor). AIS-as-input is gated behind HAXR_FEED_AIS (off by default); when on (only the veto-isolation test sets it) the test asserts ONLY arm well-formedness, never accuracy-vs-AIS, and the code+test both document the hard rule (ReplayScenarioRun.cpp HaxrScenarioRun comment; test_veto_isolation_haxr_ab.cpp header). los_guard AB does not set HAXR_FEED_AIS (radar-only vs AIS truth).
- Timestamps: kattwyk_08_t40 and kattwyk_08_dec50_w285 both monotonic non-decreasing after nothing needed (0 out-of-order rows); loaders also std::sort plots and HaxrScenarioRun sorts truth before groupTruth, so the sort-fragmentation trap is handled. AIS truth files monotonic (0 out-of-order).
- Value ranges plausible: kattwyk plots range 105-2181 m, az 0.07-359.9 deg; parkhafen rmax 2725 m; seemannshoeft rmax 4577 m (per-station radar ranges; AIS rmax slightly larger as README notes AIS-beyond-radar). No NaN/inf in the sampled plot fixtures (grep count 0).
- Cross-site window alignment for the veto AB is consistent: kattwyk/parkhafen/seemannshoeft *_08_dec50_w285.csv all span tod ~29096..29381 (~284.5 s common window). kattwyk_08_abW285.csv is byte-identical to kattwyk_08_dec50_w285.csv (harmless alias, not a defect).
- Fixtures are generated (out/ and .venv gitignored) from the DLR HDF5 via extract_plots.py; naming encodes params (t40=amp thr 40, dec50=cluster eps 50 m, w285=285 s window). Generator + validator scripts present.

Not separately reported but noted: HaxrScenarioRun (unlike PhilosScenarioRun) does not resampleTruthToClock, so the los_guard/veto AB metrics are evaluated only at native ~10-20 s AIS instants — a methodology inconsistency vs the philos path, though those tests assert only well-formedness/banded deltas so it does not corrupt a pinned number. test_occupancy_ab.cpp is in scope but does not exercise any HAXR fixture (sim + philos scenarios only).
